scenario: Pinnacle Retailer is replicating the Flowing Retail Kafka pattern on Camunda 8.8 to process 22000 orders per day. The architects want to mirror the exact technology stack from the documented example so that an incoming Kafka topic record triggers a new process instance, and so an outgoing service task publishes a new Kafka record. They need to confirm which Java framework combination is shown in the Apache Kafka section of the best-practice docs.
question: Which Java technology stack does the Flowing Retail Kafka example use to subscribe to a topic and start process instances?

options.text: Use Spring Kafka with a manual KafkaConsumer poll loop alongside the Zeebe Java client; the loop reads each record from the topic and starts a new process instance for every consumed message
Use Spring Cloud Stream Kafka Streams binder alongside the Zeebe Node.js client; the binder consumes the topic and a stateless processor function starts a new process instance for every record
Use Spring Integration Kafka channel adapters alongside the Camunda Connector runtime; the adapter routes records to an inbound connector that starts a new process instance for every record
Use Java with Spring Boot and Spring Cloud Streams as shown in the Flowing Retail example; the listener subscribes to the Kafka topic and starts a new process instance for every consumed record

a.text: Incorrect. The Apache Kafka section does not show Spring Kafka with a manual KafkaConsumer loop; the documented example uses Spring Cloud Streams on top of Spring Boot to subscribe to the topic.

b.text: Incorrect. The example is a Java stack on Spring Boot, not a Node.js client; Spring Cloud Streams is correct but the rest of the stack does not match the documented Flowing Retail example.

c.text: Incorrect. The Apache Kafka section does not route through Spring Integration channel adapters or the Connector runtime; it uses Spring Cloud Streams in a plain Java Spring Boot application.

d.text: Correct. The Apache Kafka section names Java, Spring Boot, and Spring Cloud Streams as the Flowing Retail example stack; a listener consumes the topic and starts a new process instance for every record.

explanation: In the Technology examples section, the Apache Kafka pattern uses Java with Spring Boot and Spring Cloud Streams as demonstrated in the Flowing Retail example; a Kafka listener subscribes to the topic and starts a new Camunda process instance for every incoming record.

docs.title: Connecting the workflow engine with your world
